question: What is the population of the smallest planet in the Lumina System?

answers.ground_truth: The population of the smallest planet is approximately 1.5 million.
answers.original: I do not have enough information to answer.

ops.description: The dog barked at the mailman.
The Geothermal Siphon is designed to capture and convert volcanic activity on Xylos into thermal energy.
Plorgonian dining rituals involve specific customs when eating Plorgonian stew.
The Void-Sentinels wield Silent Bows that fire arrows of Shadow-Glass, which absorbs light and sound.
Atlantean Tide-Weapons are enhanced with Current-Crystals.
Triskelion has three moons in a stable figure-8 orbit.

entities.name: Lumina System
entities.description: The Lumina System is a planetary system consisting of six planets orbiting the stable yellow dwarf known as Lumina Star.<SEP>The Lumina System is a planetary system containing multiple planets with varying conditions and unique life forms.

entities.name: Lumina Star
entities.description: Lumina Star is a stable yellow dwarf that provides consistent energy output, supporting diverse environments within the Lumina System.

entities.name: Unique Life Forms
entities.description: Unique Life Forms are diverse organisms discovered on multiple planets within the Lumina System, adapted to varying conditions.

entities.name: Verdant
entities.description: Verdant is the third planet in the Lumina System, located within the habitable zone and known for its widespread flora.<SEP>Verdant is the third planet in the Lumina System, located within the habitable zone and known for its widespread flora.

relations.description: Unique life forms have been discovered on multiple planets within the Lumina System.

Verdant is the third planet in the Lumina System, located within the habitable zone.<SEP>Verdant is the third planet in the Lumina System, located within the habitable zone and known for its widespread flora.

Siren is one of the outermost planets in the Lumina System, a distant, frozen body.<SEP>Siren is one of the outermost planets in the Lumina System, a distant, frozen body containing significant water ice deposits.

Void is one of the outermost planets in the Lumina System, a distant, frozen body.<SEP>Void is one of the outermost planets in the Lumina System, a distant, frozen body containing significant water ice deposits.
Warp gates located near orbital stations facilitate travel between planets in the Lumina System.<SEP>Warp gates located near orbital stations facilitate travel between planets in the Lumina System.
The Lumina System orbits around Lumina Star, which provides consistent energy output.
Cinder is the smallest and innermost rocky planet within the Lumina System.<SEP>Cinder is the smallest and innermost rocky planet within the Lumina System.
Aegis is the second planet in the Lumina System, serving as the mining hub.<SEP>Aegis is the second planet in the Lumina System, rich in heavy metals and serving as the system's mining hub.
Kronos is a massive gas giant dominating the outer system within the Lumina System.<SEP>Kronos is a massive gas giant that dominates the outer system within the Lumina System.
Hearth is a planet within the Lumina System with geothermal vents supporting a deep-earth civilization.
Forge is a planet within the Lumina System with an irradiated surface and shielded biodomes.

entities.name: Bio-Harmonizer
entities.description: The Bio-Harmonizer is an implant that allows for direct Neural Augmentation, improving cognitive functions.

entities.name: Whisper-Willow Tree
entities.description: The Whisper-Willow tree is known for its ethereal lightness and sheds fibers during spring.

entities.name: Station
entities.description: The station is where the train arrived.

entities.name: Triskelionian Hibernation
entities.description: Triskelionians hibernate for one month each year.

entities.name: Ice-Palace of Glacia
entities.description: Ice-Palace of Glacia is a structure that never melts.

entities.name: Temporal Society
entities.description: Temporal society exists across multiple time periods and is regulated by the Temporal-Council.
